name: Casbah of Algiers
description: UNESCO-listed old city with narrow alleys, Ottoman palaces, and mosques.

name: Martyrs' Memorial
description: Iconic concrete monument commemorating Algeria's war for independence.

name: Bardo National Museum
description: Museum with archaeology and ethnography exhibits in a former Ottoman palace.

name: Notre-Dame d’Afrique
description: 19th-century Catholic basilica overlooking the Bay of Algiers.

name: El Hamma Botanical Garden
description: Lush public garden with diverse plant species and walking paths.

name: Tipaza Roman Ruins
description: Extensive Roman city ruins on the Mediterranean coast.

name: Royal Mausoleum of Mauretania
description: Circular tomb of Berber-Roman royalty, blending Roman and native styles.

name: Mount Chenoua
description: Mountain range near Tipaza offering scenic hikes and views.

name: Tipaza Beaches
description: Sandy Mediterranean beaches with nearby cafés and Roman ruins.

name: Chréa National Park
description: Mountain park with hiking trails, cedar trees, and Barbary macaques.

name: Chréa Ski Resort
description: Seasonal ski destination in the Blida Atlas mountains.

name: Les Gorges De La Chiffa
description: Dramatic natural rock formations with scenic river views.

name: Ushuaia Beach Club
description: Clean public beaches ideal for swimming and sunbathing.

name: Corso Beach
description: Quiet, family-friendly beach with shallow waters.

name: Tibhirine Monastery
description: Peaceful monastery known for its spiritual and historical significance.

name: Hammam Righa
description: Historic thermal spring resort dating back to Roman times.

name: Emir Abdelkader’s Home
description: Historic residence of Emir Abdelkader, reflecting traditional Algerian architecture and national heritage.

name: Musée Public National de Cherchell
description: One of Algeria’s finest archaeological museums, showcasing Roman mosaics, statues, and artifacts from the region’s imperial past.

name: Al Rahman Mosque
description: A beautiful mosque in Cherchell, known for its peaceful architecture and spiritual ambiance.

name: Promenade des Sablettes
description: A scenic boardwalk in Algiers offering stunning sea views, walking paths, cafes, and leisure activities. Ideal for a family stroll or relaxing by the Mediterranean.

name: Ketchaoua Mosque
description: A historic mosque located at the foot of the Casbah in Algiers, known for its Ottoman-Baroque architecture and rich religious history.

name: Cirta Museum
description: This museum is mainly dedicated to the history of Constantine during the Roman era. There are some magnificent mosaics along with various statute and pottery.

name: Sidi M’Cid Bridge
description: The Sidi M’Cid Bridge, also known as the Suspended Bridge, is Constantine’s iconic monument, its image defining the city. It is a 164m-long suspension bridge, opened to traffic in April 1912. The bridge links the Casbah to the slopes of Sidi M’Cid hill. Views of town and the gorge, 175m below, are stunning.

name: Hippo Museum
description: Located on the ancient site of Hippo Regius, this museum displays Roman and early Christian artifacts, including pieces linked to Saint Augustine. A peaceful spot rich in history.

name: Okacha Beach
description: Sidi Akacha is part of the Chetaïbi region, a small fishing port situated on a peninsula west of Annaba. The area is rich in history and natural landscapes, offering visitors a peaceful retreat away from crowded tourist spots.

name: Tizgban Forest
description: Tizgban Forest in Skikda is a lush, evergreen haven renowned for its vibrant colors that remain stunning throughout the seasons. Its serene atmosphere and natural beauty make it a perfect destination for nature lovers and hikers seeking tranquility.​

name: Marina d’Or
description: Marina d'Or in Filfila, Skikda, is a vibrant water park offering pools, slides, and family-friendly attractions near the Mediterranean coast. It's a popular destination for both locals and tourists seeking aquatic fun.

name: Les Grottes des Singes
description: Les Grottes Merveilleuses in Jijel, Algeria, are natural limestone caves adorned with stunning stalactites and stalagmites, offering visitors a mesmerizing underground landscape. Discovered in 1917 during the construction of the RN-43 road, these caves have since become a protected natural site, attracting numerous tourists annually .​

name: Jijel-Zoo
description: Parc Animalier de Kissir, also known as the Jijel Zoo, is a renowned zoological park located in the Kissir region near El Aouana, approximately 30 km west of Jijel, Algeria. Established in 2006, it has become a significant attraction for both locals and tourists, offering a diverse range of animal species and recreational facilities.​

name: Hammam Debagh Hot Springs
description: Hammam Debagh Hot Springs in Guelma, Algeria, features mineral-rich thermal waters amid scenic mountains, offering relaxation and healing benefits. A popular natural retreat for wellness and stunning views.

name: Roman Theater of Guelma
description: A well-preserved 3rd-century AD Roman theater in Algeria, built under Emperor Septimius Severus, seating 4,500 spectators. Restored in the 20th century, it hosted plays and possibly gladiatorial combats, now a key historical attraction with statues and mosaics on display

name: Thubursicum Ruins
description: Thubursicum, located in Khemissa, Souk Ahras Province, Algeria, is an ancient Roman and Byzantine archaeological site. It features a remarkably well-preserved Roman theater, considered one of the finest in North Africa.

name: Beni Haroun Dam
description: Beni Haroun Dam in Mila Province is Algeria’s largest, supplying water to six eastern provinces and irrigating over 41,000 hectares of farmland. It has also become a popular spot for fishing and outdoor recreation.

name: Emir Abdelkader Mosque
description: The Emir Abdelkader Mosque in Constantine is Algeria's second-largest mosque, inaugurated in 1994. It features two 107-meter minarets and a grand dome, exemplifying modern Islamic architecture.

name: Viva Mall
description: Viva Mall is a modern shopping center located in Kherraza, El Bouni, near Annaba, Algeria. It features a variety of shops, restaurants, and family-friendly spaces, all within a stylish building that blends contemporary design with Moorish architecture.

name: Aftis Beach
description: Aftis Beach, located in El Aouana, Jijel Province, Algeria, is renowned for its pristine golden sands and clear turquoise waters. Surrounded by lush forests and mountains, it offers a tranquil setting for swimming, snorkeling, and camping.

name: Farouk Land Park
description: Colorful rides, including a Ferris wheel & roller coasters, plus a mini train & games for all ages.

name: Qasr Bajouda
description: A historic palace reflecting traditional Saharan architecture.

name: Assekrem Plateau
description: A breathtaking plateau in the Hoggar Mountains, famous for stunning sunsets and views. It was a retreat of Charles de Foucauld and offers visitors panoramic vistas and spiritual solitude.

name: Tahat Atakor
description: The highest mountain in Algeria is Mount Tahat, which has an elevation of 2,908 meters (9,238 feet). It is located in the Saharan Atlas range and known for rugged terrain and diverse flora and fauna.

name: Tassili N'Ajjer National Park
description: it is UNESCO World Heritage Site which has Mountainous area with ancient rock formations and prehistoric art.

name: Tamantit Palace
description: A historic ksar in the Adrar region, known for its traditional Saharan architecture. This palace is built on a high area and surrounded by a fence with a fortress locked with an iron lock.

name: Ahaggar National Park
description: A vast protected area in the Hoggar Mountains, featuring volcanic landscapes, prehistoric rock art, and diverse desert wildlife.

name: Oued Afilal
description: Afilal is a major seasonal river in the Atakor volcanic plateau, flowing year-round and nourishing the surrounding landscape.

name: Toboggan Aqua Park
description: A popular water park that offers a variety of attractions for families and visitors looking to enjoy their time in the sun.

name: Takimit n Imdochal
description: A desert region with sand dunes, rocky formations, and beautiful sunsets, perfect for photography and outdoor adventures. Many tourists enjoy camping to fully immerse in the natural environment.

name: Tin Merzouga Dunes
description: Stunning sand dunes located near the village of Merzouga, known for their magnificent landscapes rising dramatically from the surrounding desert.

name: Tin Akachaker
description: An archaeological site in the Tamanrasset region of Algeria, renowned for prehistoric rock art featuring engravings of animals and human figures illustrating ancient Saharan life.

name: Kawthar mosque
description: Al-Kawthar Mosque stands as a testament to the rich cultural and religious heritage of El Oued. It is a place where worshippers can come together in harmony, finding spiritual solace and connecting with their faith.

name: Gouri park
description: Very wide  amusemnt park with parking lots and of course a lot of games for children.

name: the Oasis of El Oued
description: known as the City of a Thousand Domes, is a stunning desert oasis in southeastern Algeria, characterized by its unique architecture and vast palm groves.

name: Moutain Peak (pic des singes)
description: Mediterranean mountain range summit & breeding ground for rare Barbary macaques.

name: Gouraya national park
description: Rugged natural expanse of mountains & limestone cliffs overlooking the sea, rich in native wildlife.

name: Casbah of Bejaia
description: Casbah of Bejaia is an old fort located in the Mediterranean port city of Bejaia, Algeria

name: Cap Carbon
description: Picturesque land mass featuring a cliffside pathway with monkeys, souvenir vendors & ocean views.

name: Bab El Fouka
description: historic gate in Béjaïa, Algeria, symbolizing the city's ancient architectural heritage and serving as a prominent landmark linking its rich past with its vibrant present.

name: Tikjda national park
description: Rich park of mountains & grassy meadows, with camp sites, hiking trails & a winter ski resort.

name: El Moudjahid museum
description: an open book on the history of the Resistance and the Algerian Revolution for freedom and dignity, offering visitors a global and detailed overview of the key stages of the armed struggle of Algerians

name: La plage rouge
description: a tranquil coastal gem renowned for its striking reddish sands, crystal-clear Mediterranean waters, and lush green surroundings,

name: Razan Mall
description: A wonderful commercial center , shpping mall and amusment park that contains all the different and varied goods, good treatment and arrangement of goods, a tight system, and setting prices.The center also contains restrooms and a prayer room, children’s toys, a shop for light and quick food, and nearby is a park for entertainment and an outlet for families in the Al-Hajar area. An experience and a project that deserves a praise

name: Waterfall Kefrida
description: a hidden gem nestled in lush greenery, offering a serene cascade of crystal-clear waters perfect for nature lovers and hikers seeking tranquility

name: Place Gueydon
description: known as Place du 1er Novembre, is a historic square in Béjaïa perched above the city’s port, offering panoramic views of the bay and serving as a vibrant cultural and social hub

name: Parc d'attraction et loisirs
description: A lively family park with rides, greenery, and entertainment for all ages in the center of Sétif.

name: Oued El Barad
description: A serene and icy mountain river flowing through pine-covered hills, ideal for picnics and peaceful nature walks.

name: El elma Dubai rue
description: A bustling commercial city famous for its wholesale markets and unbeatable deals, attracting traders from all over Algeria.

name: Djemila
description: A stunning UNESCO World Heritage Site with well-preserved Roman ruins set in the mountains, showcasing Roman town planning and culture.

name: Park Mall
description: The Commercial and Entertainment Center (Park Mall)

name: Lompi Park
description: A modern aquatic amusement park full of fun and family entertainment, perfect for hot summer days in the Aurès region.

name: Hammam Salhine
description: A historic Roman thermal spa with healing mineral waters surrounded by mountains, inviting visitors to relax in a timeless atmosphere.

name: Dream paradise park Oran
description: An ultimate family fun destination with rides, games, and relaxation for all ages. Create unforgettable memories in a lively and joyful setting.

name: Temple Minarf
description: A majestic Roman temple dedicated to the goddess Minerva, Built in the 1st century AD as part of the Roman colonial expansion in North Africa.

name: Lalla Setti Park
description: A scenic plateau overlooking Tlemcen, offering panoramic views and a tranquil environment.

name: The Remains of Mansourah
description: Historical ruins of a 13th-century city, showcasing remnants of ancient architecture.

name: The Cave of Beni Add
description: A natural cave formation near Tlemcen, known for its geological features.

name: Great Mosque of Tlemcen
description: An 11th-century mosque exemplifying Almoravid architecture.

name: El Ourit Waterfalls
description: A series of seven waterfalls set amidst pine-covered mountains near Tlemcen.

name: Marsa BenM'hidi Beach
description: A pristine beach in Marsa Ben M'Hidi, popular for its clear waters and sandy shores.

name: Aquapark Rechgoune
description: A family-friendly water park featuring various slides and pools.

name: EL Bir Beach
description: A serene beach known for its natural beauty and tranquil environment.

name: Rechgoune Beach
description: A popular beach destination offering recreational activities and scenic views.

name: Doriane Beach Club
description: An Amusement Park with water attractions suitable for all ages.

name: The Guitar Beach
description: A unique beach named for its guitar-like shape, offering a distinctive coastal experience.

name: Sidi Mohamed Lake
description: A tranquil lake surrounded by natural landscapes, ideal for relaxation.

name: Loumi Castle
description: A historical castle offering insights into the region's past and architectural heritage.

name: Santa Cruz
description: A 17th-century fort providing panoramic views of Oran and the Mediterranean Sea.

name: Ahmed Zabana National Museum
description: A museum dedicated to Algeria's history and the legacy of national hero Ahmed Zabana.

name: Ibn Badis Mosque
description: A prominent mosque in Oran known for its architectural significance.

name: The Andalusians
description: A popular beach resort near Oran known for its golden sands, turquoise waters, and family-friendly environment. It offers water sports, local food vendors, and picturesque views.

name: AZ Grand Oran
description: A popular beach resort near Oran known for its golden sands, turquoise waters, and family-friendly environment. It offers water sports, local food vendors, and picturesque views.

name: Cap Ivi Beach
description: A pristine beach known for its golden dunes and clear waters, offering a serene environment ideal for relaxation and photography.

name: Mostaland Park
description: A comprehensive Amusement Park featuring rides, a zoo, and aquatic attractions, suitable for visitors of all ages.

name: EL Asra Parc
description: A scenic public garden by the sea, offering green spaces, walking paths, and a peaceful spot for families and visitors to relax

name: Amusment Park
description: Amusment park that equiped with different setups for children and adults, that entertain them

name: The Ancient Palace of the Sons of Ali Ibn Musa
description: The shrine of Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha, with its beautiful landmarks, embraces you with its speech and text, and holds you in its warmth every time you gaze upon it, even with repetition.

name: Ksar  Beni Isguen
description: this palace is a  UNESCO World Heritage site which characterized by its traditional mud-brick construction and intricate design, showcasing the ingenuity of the local Berber people

name: Fort Polignac
description: is a historical site with significant military and architectural importance. Built during the Frenchcolonial period in the late 19th century, the fort served as a strategic military outpost

name: Ksar El Mihan
description: this palace  is associated with the tribes of Kile Taghorifet. The old center of the plateau located north of Qasar al-Mihyan belongs to Kile Ijdil, while the area to the south of the district is associated with Kile Tamamlin.

name: PLACE DE 1 NOVEMBRE
description: A historic square in Oran, known for its striking monument, elegant architecture, and central role in city life.

name: ES-SENIA
description: A modern shopping center , offering a variety of shops, restaurants, and entertainment options for visitors.

name: Aqua Souf Water Park
description: wide water par that have variety of games that could be played by kids,

name: Museum of Dar El Baroud (Museum of Idols)
description: a museu affiliated with the National Office for the Management and Exploitation of Protected Cultural Properties (Ogebc) in the middle of Chlef Province. This masterpiece  contains within its walls rare antiques and even the history of the city of Chlef, with its sources and references, through what you hear from the museum’s archaeologists.
